description: Verify multiple dimension analysts are spawned concurrently rather than sequentially
prompt: Research the edge computing market. Run all available dimensions: competitive analysis, market sizing, trend analysis, tech assessment, regulatory review.

expectations.description: Multiple dimension-analyst references indicating multiple analysts spawned
Parallel or concurrent execution pattern is used
Background execution pattern is used for analyst tasks
Analysts for independent dimensions run in parallel, not sequentially - there should be no evidence of waiting for one analyst to complete before starting the next
Maximum of 5 concurrent analysts is enforced to prevent resource exhaustion, with additional dimensions queued if more than 5 are requested

description: Verify only user-prioritized dimensions receive analyst spawns, not all available dimensions
prompt: Research the plant-based meat market. Only focus on: market sizing and trend analysis. Do NOT run other dimensions.

expectations.description: Prioritized dimensions are referenced
Trend analysis dimension is referenced
Non-requested dimensions should not appear as spawned analysts
The number of spawned analysts matches exactly the number of user-requested priorities (2 in this case), not the full set of 7 available dimensions
Non-prioritized dimensions such as competitive analysis, regulatory review, financial analysis, tech assessment, and customer research are not researched or spawned

description: Orchestrator displays framework plans per dimension in table format for user visibility
prompt: Research quantum sensors market. Run all dimensions. Show me what frameworks each analyst will use.

expectations.description: Table format with dimension-to-framework mapping is displayed
Multiple dimensions are listed with their planned frameworks
Each dimension's planned frameworks are displayed in a structured format showing the user what analytical methods each analyst will employ

description: Orchestrator includes a methodology coverage matrix in the merged report showing planned vs applied frameworks
prompt: Research sustainable aviation fuel market. After all dimensions complete, show methodology coverage.

expectations.description: Coverage matrix is present in the output
Planned vs Applied comparison is shown
Per-dimension framework status is shown
The coverage matrix compares planned frameworks (from methodology_plan) against actually applied frameworks (from findings), highlighting any gaps where a planned framework was not executed

description: Team-lead coordinates source-chunker spawning when an analyst requests chunking, routing results back to the requesting analyst
prompt: Research enterprise AI market. A dimension analyst encounters a 50-page industry report and requests chunking. Coordinate the source-chunker.

expectations.description: Source-chunker is spawned by team-lead
Results are routed back to the requesting analyst
Analyst does not spawn source-chunker directly
Team-lead receives the chunking request from the analyst, spawns the source-chunker agent, and routes the chunked findings back to the requesting analyst via SendMessage

description: Orchestrator handles methodology plan timeout gracefully by logging a warning and continuing with gap notation
prompt: Research edge AI market. One analyst fails to produce a methodology plan within timeout. How does orchestration handle this?

expectations.description: Warning is logged for the timeout
Session continues rather than aborting
Gap is noted for the missing methodology plan

When a methodology plan is not written within the expected timeout, the orchestrator logs a warning, marks that dimension's methodology as unverified, and continues the session rather than aborting the entire research run

description: Two topics in same project use independent configs from their respective topics[] entries
prompt: /sigint:start --topic topic-a (both topic-a and topic-b have entries in sigint.config.json with different maxDimensions)

expectations.description: Only topic-a config values are used, not topic-b values
Research orchestrator is invoked with the correct scope
